ANSWER TO COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 76644272) FILED BY DEFENDANT CENTER STREET LENDING VIII SPE, LLC ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS CENTER STREET LENDING VII

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT PURSUANT TO CCP 664.6. ARGUED. THE COURT MODIFIES ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED TO JULY 30, 2025. COUNSEL TO ENSURE THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES, INCLUDING THE RESPONSE FILED ON JULY 16, 2025. FURTHER, DEFENDANT IS ALLOWED TO FILE A REPLY, DUE 5 DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER ENFORCING WRITTEN SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT, AND/OR FOR AN INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED, IN PART, AS TO THE REQUEST FOR LIQUATED DAMAGES (SECTION 12 OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT). PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO THE SUM OF $50,000 PREVIOUSLY DEPOSITED IN ESCROW FOR LIQUIDATED DAMAGES AS A RESULT OF DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO REMOVE EDRI FROM THE FILBERT STREET LOAN AS REQUIRED BY SECTION 4(A), BY THE FEBRUARY 14, 2025 CLOSE OF ESCROW DEADLINE. PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR JUDGMENT AND FOR PARTITION ARE DENIED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING.) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: BRIANNA RUDD, CSR 13668 (T: 209.747.1306, E: BRIANNARUDD@GMAIL.COM)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS IDAN SOFIOV AND TAMAR RACHEL KASSOFF-SOFIOV'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED, IN PART. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR ENFORCE SECTIONS 2 AND 3 OF THE AGREEMENT, DISMISSAL OF PARTITION ACTION, IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED. GIVEN THE COMPETING MOTIONS TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND THE RELIEF GRANTED TO EACH PARTY, THE COURT DOES NOT FIND A PREVAILING PARTY UNDER SECTION 18 OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. ORDER SUBMITTED AT THE HEARING IS UNDER REVIEW. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: BRIANNA RUDD, CSR 13668 (T: 209.747.1306, E: BRIANNARUDD@GMAIL.COM)

Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedures 664.6 And Supporting Declarations
Off Calendar Jul-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS IDAN SOFIOV AND TAMAR RACHEL KASSOFF-SOFIOV'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT

Appearances: Ellis Ross Anderson of Anderson & Poole (415.956.6413) for plaintiff Avi Edri; William Klein of the Klein Law Group (415.693.9107) for defendants Idan Sofiov and Tamar Rachel Kassoff-Sofiov; Kelsey Luu of Best Best & Krieger LLP (949.263.2618) for defendant Center Street Lending VIII SPE, LLC.

This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED, in part. Defendant may use a promissory note as part of the $600,000 Sofiov Consideration, under Section 4(c) of the Settlement Agreement. Contrary to Plaintiff's assertions, neither subdivision (a) nor (c) of Section 4, or any other term or provision in the Settlement Agreement, conditions the right to pay by promissory note upon a deadline.

Defendant's request for enforce Sections 2 and 3 of the Agreement, Dismissal of Partition Action, is denied. It is undisputed that Plaintiff complied with Section 2(b)(iii), which requires Plaintiff to execute and deliver to escrow a Request for Dismissal of this action. Section 3 provides that Escrow file the Dismissal. Section 3 cannot be enforced against Plaintiff as written. Defendant's request for attorney's fees is denied. Given the competing motions to enforce the settlement agreement and the relief granted to each party, the Court does not find a prevailing party under Section 18 of the Settlement Agreement. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Brianna Rudd, CSR 13668 (T: 209.747.1306, E: Briannarudd@gmail.com) (501/GLW)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Enforcing Written Settlement Agreement, And/Or For An Interlocut0ry Judgment For Partition And Sale Of Real Property And For Appointment Of Partition Referee
Off Calendar Jul-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER ENFORCING WRITTEN SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT, AND/OR FOR AN INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT

Appearances: Ellis Ross Anderson of Anderson & Poole (415.956.6413) for plaintiff Avi Edri; William Klein of the Klein Law Group (415.693.9107) for defendants Idan Sofiov and Tamar Rachel Kassoff-Sofiov; Kelsey Luu of Best Best & Krieger LLP (949.263.2618) for defendant Center Street Lending VIII SPE, LLC.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED, in part, as to the request for Liquated Damages (Section 12 of the Settlement Agreement). Plaintiff is entitled to the sum of $50,000 previously deposited in Escrow for liquidated damages as a result of Defendant's failure to remove Edri from the Filbert Street loan as required by Section 4(a), by the February 14, 2025 Close of Escrow deadline.

Plaintiff's requests for judgment and for partition are denied. While it is undisputed that Defendant missed the February 14, 2025 Close of Escrow deadline, the Court finds that under the circumstances, there was a mutual intent to continue performing under the agreement, such that the missed deadline was not material breach of the agreement. The evidence shows the parties were in regular contact from March until June 2025, when Defendant was finally able to obtain financing. During this time, Defendant was providing assurances to Plaintiff that they were still proceeding in good faith to obtain financing. Defendant also performed a central obligation under the Agreement by quit-claiming his interest in the States Street property, conferring a substantial value on Plaintiff. As for Plaintiff, the evidence shows Plaintiff was still willing to perform under the agreement, despite the missed deadline, provided that Defendant tender the $600,000 in cash, as opposed to a combination of cash and a promissory note. Finally, the parties' agreement provides the remedy for the missed deadline in the form of a liquidated damages provision. The court grants plaintiff's request to delay enforcement of the order by two weeks. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Brianna Rudd, CSR 13668 (T: 209.747.1306, E: Briannarudd@gmail.com) (501/GLW)
